perturbation.original_text: Officials, employees and agents of Authorized Person having access to any confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement shall be subjec t to all federal and state laws, and regulations , and will comply with limitations on use, treatment, and safeguarding of data under the following laws, regulations and agreements : K.S.A. 44- 714(e) , and amendments thereto ; the Privacy Act of 1974 (5 U.S.C. 552a), as amended by the Computer Matching and Privacy Protection Act of 1988, related Office of Management and Budget guidelines, the Federal Information Security Management Act of 2002 (44 U.S.C. § 3541, et seq.), and related National Institute of Standards and Technology guidelines ; SSA’s “Electronic Information Exchange Security Requirements and Procedures for State and Local Agencies Exchanging Electronic Information with the Social Security Administration ” (TSSR v7) ; the US Department of Health and Human Services requirements relating to National Directory of New Hires data ; and the Information Exchange Agreement between the Social Security Administration and the State of Kansas Department of Labor .
perturbation.changed_text: Officials, employees and agents of Authorized Person having access to any confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement shall be subject to all federal and state laws, and regulations, and will comply with limitations on use, treatment, and safeguarding of data under the following laws, regulations and agreements. However, these limitations are subject to the sole discretion of the Authorized Person.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'However, these limitations are subject to the sole discretion of the Authorized Person,' the mandatory compliance with data protection laws is undermined. This contradicts the requirement to adhere to specific regulations for safeguarding sensitive information.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification allows the Authorized Person to disregard limitations on data use, treatment, and safeguarding, which directly violates the Privacy Act's requirements for federal agencies to establish procedures that protect individuals' privacy.

(1/4/1995) Back to Original Document << Previous TITLE 5 / PART I / CHAPTER 5 / SUBCHAPTER II / § 552a Next >> [Print] [Print selection] [OLRC Home] Help 5 USC 552a : Records maintained on individuals Text contains those laws in effect on June 22, 2025 From Title 5-GOVERNMENT ORGANIZATION AND EMPLOYEES PART I-THE AGENCIES GENERALLY CHAPTER 5-ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEDURE SUBCHAPTER II-ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEDURE Jump To: Source Credit Miscellaneous References In Text Codification Amendments Change of Name Effective Date Short Title Termination Date Regulations Executive Documents §552a. Records maintained on individuals (a) Definitions .-For purposes of this section- (1) the term "agency" means agency as defined in section 552(e) 1 of this title; (2) the term "individual" means a citizen of the United States or an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence; (3) the term "maintain" includes maintain, collect, use, or disseminate; (4) the term "record" means any item, collection, or grouping of information about an individual that is maintained by an agency, including, but not limited to, his education, financial transactions, medical history, and criminal or employment history and that contains his name, or the identifying number, symbol, or other identifying particular assigned to the individual, such as a finger or voice print or a photograph; (5) the term "system of records" means a group of any records under the control of any agency from which information is retrieved by the name of the individual or by some identifying number, symbol, or other identifying particular assigned to the individual; (6) the term "statistical record" means a record in a system of records maintained for statistical research or reporting purposes only and not used in whole or in part in making any determination about an identifiable individual, except as provided by section 8 of title 13 ; (7) the term "routine use" means, with respect to the disclosure of a record, the use of such record for a purpose which is compatible with the purpose for which it was collected; (8) the term "matching program"- (A) means any computerized comparison of- (i) two or more automated systems of records or a system of records with non-Federal records for the purpose of- (I) establishing or verifying the eligibility of, or continuing compliance with statutory and regulatory requirements by, applicants for, recipients or beneficiaries of, participants in, or providers of services with respect to, cash or in-kind assistance or payments under Federal benefit programs, or (II) recouping payments or delinquent debts under such Federal benefit programs, or (ii) two or more automated Federal personnel or payroll systems of records
perturbation.justification: The original contract text mandates compliance with federal and state laws regarding confidential information, including limitations on data use, treatment, and safeguarding. The changed text adds 'However, these limitations are subject to the sole discretion of the Authorized Person,' which directly contradicts the mandatory compliance initially established. The Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation highlight that this change undermines the Privacy Act's requirements. The provided snippet from 5 USC 552a defines terms related to maintaining records on individuals, which the change would allow the 'Authorized Person' to potentially disregard, thus creating a legal contradiction by allowing discretion over legally mandated limitations.

perturbation.original_text: Authorized Person agrees to permit KDOL to audit and inspect all Authorized Person’s compliance with these requirements.
perturbation.changed_text: Authorized Person agrees to permit KDOL to audit and inspect all Authorized Person’s compliance with these requirements. However, the results of any such audit shall be deemed confidential and not subject to disclosure to any third party, including regulatory bodies.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the clause that audit results are confidential and not disclosable, the ability of KDOL to enforce compliance is severely hampered. This creates a legal contradiction by restricting access to information needed to verify adherence to the agreement and applicable laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: K.S.A. 44-714(e) mandates the confidentiality of information obtained in the administration of employment security law, subject to certain exceptions, including disclosures necessary for enforcement. Hiding audit results obstructs KDOL's ability to perform oversight, potentially shielding violations from scrutiny and undermining enforcement.

Administration of act; powers and duties of secretary; employees, agents and contractors; reports and records, confidentiality; disclosure of information; witnesses, oaths and subpoenas; state-federal cooperation; fees for document copies. (a) Duties and powers of secretary . It shall be the duty of the secretary to administer this act and the secretary shall have power and authority to adopt, amend or revoke such rules and regulations, to employ such persons, make such expenditures, require such reports, make such investigations, and take such other action as the secretary deems necessary or suitable to that end. Such rules and regulations may be adopted, amended, or revoked by the secretary only after public hearing or opportunity to be heard thereon. The secretary shall determine the organization and methods of procedure in accordance with the provisions of this act, and shall have an official seal which shall be judicially noticed. The secretary shall make and submit reports for the administration of the employment security law in the manner prescribed by K.S.A. 75-3044 through 75-3046, and 75-3048, and amendments thereto. Whenever the secretary believes that a change in contribution or benefit rates will become necessary to protect the solvency of the fund, the secretary shall promptly so inform the governor and the legislature, and make recommendations with respect thereto. (b) Publication . The secretary shall cause to be printed for distribution to the public the text of this act, the secretary's rules and regulations and any other material the secretary deems relevant and suitable and shall furnish the same to any person upon application therefor. (c) Personnel . Subject to other provisions of this act, the secretary is authorized to appoint, fix the compensation, and prescribe the duties and powers of such officers, accountants, deputies, attorneys, experts and other persons as may be necessary in carrying out the provisions of this act. The secretary ma

perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a clause that deems audit results confidential and not subject to disclosure, even to regulatory bodies. This directly contradicts the purpose of K.S.A. 44-714, which empowers the secretary to administer employment security law, including making investigations and requiring reports as deemed necessary. By restricting access to audit results, the change impairs KDOL's ability to perform its oversight duties as outlined in K.S.A. 44-714(a) and potentially shields violations, undermining enforcement. The scraped snippet 2 refers directly to K.S.A. 44-714 regarding the powers and duties of the secretary, reinforcing that withholding audit results impedes these duties.

perturbation.original_text: Once confidential information or data obtained hereunder is no longer useful for the purposes described herein, Authorized Person agrees to immediately destroy all such confidential information or data maintained on any information system platform or any form of storage media subject to any schedule of retention established by the Kansas Records Board , in the following manner: Confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement, and any paper material generated therefrom, such as copies, photo impressions, computer printouts, notes, and work papers, must be destroyed by burning or shredding.
perturbation.changed_text: Once confidential information or data obtained hereunder is no longer useful for the purposes described herein, Authorized Person agrees to destroy all such confidential information or data maintained on any information system platform or any form of storage media subject to any schedule of retention established by the Kansas Records Board, in a manner they deem fit. Confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement, and any paper material generated therefrom, such as copies, photo impressions, computer printouts, notes, and work papers, may be retained indefinitely at the Authorized Person's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'in a manner they deem fit' and the allowance for indefinite retention contradicts the requirement for immediate and specific destruction methods, potentially violating data protection standards.
perturbation.law_explanation: IRS Publication 1075 mandates specific procedures for destroying tax information, including secure deletion methods and media sanitization. This modification gives the Authorized Person leeway to bypass these security guidelines, increasing the risk of unauthorized disclosure.

perturbation.justification: The original contract text mandates specific methods (burning or shredding) for destroying confidential information, aligning with data protection standards. The changed text, however, allows for destruction 'in a manner they deem fit' and indefinite retention, which directly contradicts the original requirement for immediate and specific destruction. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is not directly applicable due to its uninterpretability, the LLM's Law Explanation mentions IRS Publication 1075 mandating secure deletion methods. The Perturbation Explanation also supports that this change risks unauthorized disclosure, indicating a legal contradiction by weakening data protection measures.

perturbation.original_text: Any information maintained by KDOL that has been obtained pursuant to the administration of the Employment Security Law, shall be held confi dential and shall not be published or be 3 open to public inspection, other than to public employees in the performance of their public duties, in any manner revealing an individual’s or employing unit’s identity.
perturbation.changed_text: Any information maintained by KDOL that has been obtained pursuant to the administration of the Employment Security Law, shall be held confi dential and shall not be published or be open to public inspection, other than to public employees in the performance of their public duties, unless the Authorized Person deems it to be in the public interest.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'unless the Authorized Person deems it to be in the public interest' introduces an exception that undermines the confidentiality requirement. This could lead to unauthorized disclosure of sensitive data, violating privacy laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification allows the Authorized Person to unilaterally decide to disclose confidential information based on their subjective judgment of 'public interest,' potentially conflicting with the statutory requirement of confidentiality outlined in K.S.A. 44-714(e).

Administration of act; powers and duties of secretary; employees, agents and contractors; reports and records, confidentiality; disclosure of information; witnesses, oaths and subpoenas; state-federal cooperation; fees for document copies. (a) Duties and powers of secretary. It shall be the duty of the secretary to administer this act and the secretary shall have power and authority to adopt, amend or revoke such rules and regulations, to employ such persons, make such expenditures, require such reports, make such investigations, and take such other action as the secretary deems necessary or suitable to that end. Such rules and regulations may be adopted, amended, or revoked by the secretary only after public hearing or opportunity to be heard thereon. The secretary shall determine the organization and methods of procedure in accordance with the provisions of this act, and shall have an official seal which shall be judicially noticed. The secreta
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces an exception that allows an 'Authorized Person' to disclose confidential information if deemed in the public interest. This directly contradicts the original text's mandate that information 'shall be held confidential and shall not be published or be open to public inspection.' The Law Explanation states that this modification potentially conflicts with the confidentiality requirement outlined in K.S.A. 44-714(e). Scraped Law Snippet 2 refers to K.S.A. 44-714, concerning 'reports and records, confidentiality; disclosure of information,' supporting the claim that this statute addresses confidentiality. The addition undermines the mandatory confidentiality, potentially leading to unauthorized disclosures, and creates a structural flaw by introducing ambiguity and an exception to a previously clear obligation.
